title: Describe Image Purpose, Not Pixel Inventory
summary: Alternative text communicates an image’s purpose in context. Informative images need a concise equivalent; functional images name the action; complex images need a nearby extended explanation; and decorative images use an empty alt. Intrinsic width and height reserve the correct aspect ratio before the image loads. Predict, inspect, explain, and apply the contract in a cumulative public-information build.
objectives: Explain html images alt with source and browser evidence.
Apply html images alt without discarding earlier document work.
Diagnose the named misconception in a changed public-service scenario.

steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which claim should guide the first clinic photo and map audit decision before the page is opened? Work context: clinic photo and map audit.
steps.why: Describe Image Purpose, Not Pixel Inventory requires this decision because the learner must distinguish a specification-defined relationship from a browser-recovered appearance. This is checkpoint 1 in the clinic photo and map audit.

options.text: Clinic photo and map audit case 1: omitting alt is the recommended way to mark an image decorative.
Clinic photo and map audit case 1: classify the image purpose first, then provide the shortest equivalent that preserves that purpose.
Clinic photo and map audit case 1: the filename is always an adequate alternative because it identifies the asset.

options.text: clinic photo and map audit phase 2.1: determine whether the image is informative, functional, complex, or decorative
clinic photo and map audit phase 2.2: write or omit verbal content according to that purpose
clinic photo and map audit phase 2.3: provide intrinsic dimensions to reserve its aspect ratio
clinic photo and map audit phase 2.4: test the surrounding content with the image unavailable
